title: melampyrum arvense subsp. pseudobarbatum
intro: Melampyrum arvense subsp. pseudobarbatum, also known as yellow fumitory, is a flowering plant species in the family Scrophulariaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its reported medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing anxiety, and promoting digestion. Melampyrum arvense subsp. pseudobarbatum is traditionally prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can also be used fresh in salads, although its efficacy and safety as a food source are not well-documented.
